AEBP2.function: Accessory component of the Polycomb Repressive Complex 2 (PRC2) that catalyzes histone H3K27 trimethylation. Regulates epigenetic silencing of developmental and tumor suppressor genes.
drugs: Tazemetostat (EZH2 inhibitor, FDA-approved for other cancers, targets PRC2 catalytic subunit)
AEBP2.hccRole: Enhances PRC2-mediated silencing of tumor suppressor genes in HCC. Connects hypoxia-driven epigenetic reprogramming to long-term transcriptional changes that sustain tumor phenotype.
AEBP2.keyFinding: Overexpression correlates with poor OS in HCC (HR=1.271, 95% CI 1.058-1.525, p=0.01); promotes PRC2-mediated tumor suppressor silencing

BACH1.function: Transcriptional repressor that competes with Nrf2 for ARE binding. Suppresses antioxidant gene expression and promotes heme-regulated gene programs.
drugs: HMOX1 inducers (hemin) degrade BACH1 via heme binding
HPP-4382 (BACH1 inhibitor, preclinical, being studied in breast cancer metastasis)
BACH1.hccRole: Context-dependent role in HCC: represses antioxidant genes but promotes metastasis by activating EMT and invasion programs. BACH1 can also promote metabolic reprogramming toward glycolysis in metastatic HCC.
BACH1.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.193, 95% CI 1.037-1.373, p=0.014); promotes metastasis by activating HK2 and GAPDH glycolytic genes

CDC42.function: Small Rho-family GTPase controlling cytoskeletal dynamics, cell polarity, and migration. Regulates actin polymerization and filopodia formation.
CDC42.hccRole: Promotes HCC invasion and metastasis by reorganizing the actin cytoskeleton. Hypoxia-induced CDC42 activation enables tumor cell migration through extracellular matrix.
CDC42.keyFinding: High expression associates with poor OS in HCC (HR=1.283, 95% CI 1.101-1.495, p=0.001); promotes portal vein invasion

EPO.function: Glycoprotein hormone that stimulates red blood cell production in response to hypoxia. Canonical HIF target gene and the classical marker of oxygen-sensing pathway activation.
drugs: Recombinant EPO (epoetin alfa - used for anemia, may be contraindicated in cancer)
EPO.hccRole: HIF-1/2 alpha target gene; autocrine EPO-EPOR signaling in HCC promotes proliferation, angiogenesis, and resistance to hypoxia. Liver is an alternative EPO production site.
EPO.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.212, 95% CI 1.056-1.391, p=0.006); EPO-EPOR axis promotes HCC angiogenesis

G6PD.function: Rate-limiting enzyme of the pentose phosphate pathway. Generates NADPH, essential for glutathione recycling and lipid biosynthesis.
G6PD.hccRole: Provides the NADPH supply that fuels both antioxidant defense and lipid synthesis in rapidly growing HCC. Upregulation correlates with poor prognosis and therapy resistance.
G6PD.keyFinding: Overexpression predicts poor OS in HCC (HR=1.371, 95% CI 1.229-1.528, p<0.001); links metabolic reprogramming to redox homeostasis

GC.function: Major plasma carrier of vitamin D metabolites. Also functions as a macrophage activating factor (GcMAF) and actin scavenger after tissue injury.
drugs: Vitamin D analogs (calcitriol, paricalcitol) - indirect pathway activation
GC.hccRole: Protective factor with HR < 1 (0.763). Liver-synthesized protein whose loss reflects hepatocyte dysfunction. Low GC expression correlates with advanced HCC stage and impaired macrophage-mediated tumor clearance.
GC.keyFinding: Protective in HCC (HR=0.763, 95% CI 0.635-0.918, p=0.004); low expression marks poor differentiation

GLRX2.function: Mitochondrial glutaredoxin that maintains iron-sulfur cluster integrity and protects against oxidative damage in mitochondria.
GLRX2.hccRole: Maintains mitochondrial redox balance in HCC. Overexpression protects tumor mitochondria from oxidative damage and supports iron-sulfur cluster biogenesis needed for tumor metabolism.
GLRX2.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.224, 95% CI 1.045-1.433, p=0.012); mitochondrial redox protector enabling tumor survival

GRB2.function: Adaptor protein linking receptor tyrosine kinases (RTKs) to the Ras-MAPK signaling cascade. Essential for growth factor signal transduction.
GRB2.hccRole: Amplifies proliferative signaling in HCC. Connects hypoxia-driven growth factor release to downstream MAPK activation, linking oxygen sensing to tumor cell division.
GRB2.keyFinding: Overexpression correlates with poor OS in HCC (HR=1.342, 95% CI 1.148-1.568, p<0.001)

GSR.function: Reduces oxidized glutathione (GSSG) back to reduced glutathione (GSH) using NADPH. Maintains the intracellular GSH pool critical for detoxification and antioxidant defense.
drugs: BCNU/Carmustine (GSR inhibitor, FDA-approved for brain tumors)
GSR.hccRole: Maintains the GSH/GSSG ratio in HCC, sustaining the glutathione antioxidant shield that protects tumor cells from ROS damage and ferroptosis.
GSR.keyFinding: Overexpression correlates with poor OS in HCC (HR=1.339, 95% CI 1.142-1.571, p<0.001); high GSR sustains ferroptosis resistance

HK2.function: Phosphorylates glucose to glucose-6-phosphate, the first committed step of glycolysis. Localizes to the outer mitochondrial membrane, coupling glycolysis to mitochondrial function.
HK2.hccRole: Rate-limiting glycolytic enzyme overexpressed in HCC. Binds mitochondrial VDAC to inhibit apoptosis while fueling the Warburg effect. HIF-1alpha transcriptional target.
HK2.keyFinding: Overexpression predicts poor OS in HCC (HR=1.287, 95% CI 1.116-1.485, p=0.001); silencing inhibits HCC growth in xenograft models

HMOX1.function: Catalyzes oxidative cleavage of heme to biliverdin, carbon monoxide, and free iron. Central regulator of oxidative stress defense, ferroptosis resistance, and inflammatory modulation in the tumor microenvironment.
HMOX1.hccRole: Dual role as both altitude adaptation (HIF-1 target) and ferroptosis regulator. Releases free iron from heme (pro-ferroptotic) but generates cytoprotective CO and biliverdin (anti-ferroptotic). Overexpression in HCC promotes tumor survival under oxidative stress.
HMOX1.keyFinding: The overlap gene between both signatures; risk factor in HCC (HR=1.148, 95% CI 1.033-1.276, p=0.01); links hypoxia adaptation to redox homeostasis

LDHA.function: Converts pyruvate to lactate under anaerobic conditions. The canonical marker of the Warburg effect and glycolytic metabolism in tumors.
LDHA.hccRole: Highest LASSO coefficient (0.147) in the altitude signature. Drives aerobic glycolysis in HCC, producing lactate that acidifies the tumor microenvironment and suppresses anti-tumor immunity.
LDHA.keyFinding: High LDHA predicts poor OS in HCC (HR=1.343, 95% CI 1.160-1.554, p<0.001)

MAFG.function: Small Maf protein that heterodimerizes with Nrf2 to activate antioxidant response elements (AREs). Coordinates transcription of detoxification and antioxidant genes under oxidative stress.
MAFG.hccRole: Second highest hazard ratio (1.654) among all 20 genes. Drives Nrf2-dependent antioxidant programs that shield HCC cells from ferroptosis and chemotherapy-induced ROS.
MAFG.keyFinding: Highest HR in ROS/ferroptosis signature (HR=1.654, 95% CI 1.401-1.954, p<0.001); MAFG amplification drives NRF2-dependent chemoresistance

MSRA.function: Repairs oxidized methionine residues in proteins, reversing oxidative damage. Acts as both a protein repair enzyme and an antioxidant scavenger of ROS.
MSRA.hccRole: Uniquely protective: the only gene in the ROS signature with HR < 1 (0.725). High MSRA expression associates with better survival, suggesting it restrains tumor progression through oxidative damage repair.
MSRA.keyFinding: Only protective gene in ROS/ferroptosis signature (HR=0.725, 95% CI 0.587-0.896, p=0.003); low expression marks aggressive HCC

NCF2.function: Component of the NADPH oxidase complex that generates superoxide radicals. Part of the innate immune defense system for pathogen killing.
drugs: GKT137831 (NOX1/4 inhibitor, clinical trials for fibrosis)
NCF2.hccRole: Component of NOX2 NADPH oxidase in tumor-infiltrating immune cells. Paradoxically, NCF2-driven ROS in the tumor microenvironment can promote HCC through chronic inflammation and DNA damage.
NCF2.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.246, 95% CI 1.096-1.417, p=0.001); reflects pro-tumorigenic inflammation in liver microenvironment

SENP1.function: De-SUMOylation enzyme that removes SUMO modifications from target proteins, including HIF-1alpha. Regulates protein stability and transcriptional activity under hypoxia.
SENP1.hccRole: Stabilizes HIF-1alpha by removing SUMO tags that mark it for degradation. SENP1 overexpression in HCC amplifies the hypoxic transcriptional response even under mild oxygen deprivation.
SENP1.keyFinding: Overexpression correlates with poor OS in HCC (HR=1.329, 95% CI 1.122-1.574, p=0.001); promotes HIF-1alpha stability

SLC7A11.function: Cystine/glutamate antiporter that imports cystine for glutathione synthesis. The primary gatekeeper of cysteine supply for the glutathione-GPX4 ferroptosis defense axis.
SLC7A11.hccRole: Master regulator of ferroptosis resistance in HCC. Imports cystine for GSH synthesis, directly suppressing lipid peroxidation and ferroptotic cell death. Key therapeutic target.
SLC7A11.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.218, 95% CI 1.100-1.348, p<0.001); sorafenib induces ferroptosis partly through SLC7A11 inhibition in HCC

SQSTM1.function: Selective autophagy receptor that targets ubiquitinated cargo for degradation. Also activates Nrf2 by competitively binding KEAP1, linking autophagy to antioxidant defense.
SQSTM1.hccRole: Accumulates in autophagy-deficient HCC, constitutively activating Nrf2 and driving antioxidant gene expression. Creates a feedforward loop protecting tumor cells from ROS-mediated death.
SQSTM1.keyFinding: Risk factor in HCC (HR=1.336, 95% CI 1.184-1.507, p<0.001); p62-KEAP1-NRF2 axis is a validated oncogenic driver in hepatocarcinogenesis

TXNRD1.function: Reduces thioredoxin using NADPH, maintaining cellular redox balance. Key enzyme in the thioredoxin antioxidant system, protecting cells from oxidative damage and regulating redox-sensitive transcription factors.
drugs: Auranofin (FDA-approved for rheumatoid arthritis, potent TXNRD1 inhibitor being repurposed)
Ethaselen (TXNRD1 inhibitor, clinical trials in China)
TXNRD1.hccRole: Highest hazard ratio (1.586) in the ROS signature. Overexpression enables HCC cells to tolerate high ROS levels generated during rapid proliferation and hypoxia.
TXNRD1.keyFinding: Strongest risk factor in ROS/ferroptosis signature (HR=1.586, 95% CI 1.364-1.844, p<0.001); auranofin sensitizes HCC to sorafenib
